relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Political violence becomes the occasion for discernment.
Jesus refuses to read suffering as proof of greater guilt.
The urgent call is turning and realignment.
Disaster at Siloam also does not prove special blame.
All are called into turning.
A fruitless fig tree is sought in the vineyard.
Repeated fruitlessness raises the question of judgment.
Mercy asks for one more season of cultivation.
The future still asks for fruit.
Sabbath teaching becomes the setting of release.
A bent woman has carried weakness for eighteen years.
Jesus sees her and speaks release.
Touch straightens her into glory.
Religious indignation objects to healing on Sabbath.
Jesus exposes the mercy already given to animals on Sabbath.
A daughter of Abraham must be loosed on Sabbath.
Opposition is shamed while the crowd rejoices.
The reign asks for comparison.
Mustard grows from small seed into shelter.
Again the reign is compared.
A woman hides leaven until the whole is transformed.
Jesus keeps teaching on the Jerusalem road.
Someone asks about how many are being saved/healed.
Jesus answers with the narrow door.
A shut door cannot be opened by late familiarity.
Eating near him and hearing him nearby is not the same as alignment.
The workers of injustice are not known.
The patriarchs and prophets are seen inside the reign.
People come from every direction to recline in the reign.
First and last are reversed.
Herod’s threat meets Jesus’ resolve.
Healing and release continue until the appointed completion.
The prophet road still leads toward Jerusalem.
Jesus laments Jerusalem with mother-hen tenderness.
The house is left until the coming one is blessed.
